scope: Phase 6e Wave 1 - Stage 9 LLM figure review of fig_a2_vs_linearized_G_N (Heat Kernel Expansion / Decision Gate E.2)
note_on_previous_report: Earlier report covering fig_bhl_bilocal_correction / fig_ew_transition_phase_diagram / fig_higgs_mass_parameter_scan superseded by this Phase 6e Wave 1 single-figure review. Prior verdicts on those Phase 5z figures unchanged and recoverable from git history.

figures.name: fig_a2_vs_linearized_G_N

issues: Left panel x-axis (log α_ADW): Plotly is rendering BOTH major and minor tick labels, producing a confusing tick string '5, 0.1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 2' instead of clean major-only labels at {0.1, 1, 10}. Recommend setting `dtick=1` (one decade per major tick) or `tickvals=[0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5]` with `tickformat` on the x-axis update at src/core/visualizations.py:10097-10101.

Left panel: the V-shaped relative-error curve clips the top of the panel at the left edge (rel_err > 1.05 for very small alpha_ADW) and exits the panel without indication of where it is going. Either extend y-range to ~[0, 2] OR add an annotation 'rel err diverges as alpha_ADW -> 0' near the top-left where the curve leaves the frame.

Right panel x-axis: tick labels are rendered with mixed SI-prefix abbreviations and scientific notation: '10B, 1T, 100T, 10^16, 10^18'. '10B' = 10^10 GeV and '1T' = 10^12 GeV are the kaleido SI prefixer kicking in - inappropriate for a physics figure where Lambda_UV runs over decades. Force `exponentformat='power'` and `tickformat=''` (or `'~e'`) on the right-panel x-axis (src/core/visualizations.py:10157-10161) to get uniform '10^10, 10^12, ..., 10^18' labels.

Right panel y-axis: similar SI-prefix-vs-power mixing on tick labels ('1u, 1p, 10^-18, 10^-24, 10^-30, ...'). Force `exponentformat='power'` on the y-axis at src/core/visualizations.py:10162-10166 for consistent '10^-N' labeling.

Right panel y-axis title: source code reads `'G_N [GeV-2]'` (Unicode superscript-minus + 2) but in the rendered PNG the superscript-minus appears to be stripped by kaleido's font fallback - the label visually reads 'GeV^2' which is the WRONG dimensional unit. Replace with ASCII `'G_N [GeV^-2]'` or HTML `'G_N [GeV<sup>-2</sup>]'` to guarantee correct rendering.

Right panel: the M_Pl annotation text 'M_Pl ~= 1.22 x 10^19 GeV' sits at xanchor='right' against the dotted Planck-mass vertical line, putting the text very close to the panel right edge. Consider moving the annotation to xanchor='left' just to the left of the M_Pl line, or shrinking the x-axis upper bound to 5e19 to give the label breathing room.

Left panel: V-shape correctly hits zero at alpha_ADW = 1 (Sakharov-Adler exact baseline). Verified against a2_calibration_relative_error formula in src/heat_kernel/a2_computation.py:40-50: rel_err = |1 - alpha| / alpha, which gives exact zero at alpha=1, ~0.5 at alpha=2, and diverges as alpha->0 - the rendered curve matches.

Pass-band [0, 0.5] correctly aligned with HEAT_KERNEL_PARAMS['A2_GN_MATCH_TOLERANCE'] = 0.5 (constants.py:2392). Natural-parameter band alpha_ADW in [0.5, 1.5] sits inside the green pass region as the docstring claims.

Right panel: log-log slope is monotonic and visually consistent with G_N proportional to 1/Lambda_UV^2 (Sakharov scaling). At Lambda_UV = M_Pl = 1.22e19 GeV with N_f = 15, G_N_from_a2 = 12*pi/(15*(1.22e19)^2) ~= 1.7e-38 GeV^-2; CODATA reference is 6.7e-39 GeV^-2. The two lines do NOT cross in the rendered panel - they sit within a factor ~2.5 at Lambda_UV = M_Pl. This is physically correct because the figure uses N_f = 15 (one generation of quarks-only or anchor convention), whereas an exact CODATA crossing would require N_f = 45 (three generations no nu_R). The docstring statement 'The curve crosses the observed line near Lambda_UV ~= M_Pl' is slightly overstated for N_f=15: the curve approaches but does not cross. Either clarify the docstring + figure caption to say 'approaches within a factor ~3' OR re-anchor the figure to N_f = 45 for a true crossing.
Planck-mass vertical anchor at LAMBDA_UV_PLANCK_GEV correctly placed at the right edge of the x-range as expected.
CODATA reference line G_N_OBS_GEV_M2 ~= 6.71e-39 GeV^-2 placed at correct y-position.

Color palette is on-brand: steel_blue (#2E86AB) for the main curves, amber (#F18F01) for Sakharov-Adler reference + CODATA dashed line, emerald for the pass-band shading, horizon (black) dotted for the Planck-mass anchor. Colorblind-safe (no red/green together).

Master title 'Phase 6e Wave 1 - Seeley-DeWitt a_2 calibration to Sakharov-Adler G_N (Decision Gate E.2)' renders cleanly above subplot titles with margin t=110 (visualizations.py:10178). No title/subplot crowding.
Two-panel layout (column_widths=[0.55, 0.45], horizontal_spacing=0.18) gives both panels enough width; no overlap between left-panel right edge and right-panel left edge.

figures.notes: Physics content is correct - this is a publishable Decision Gate E.2 calibration figure. Two non-blocking style/rendering polish issues remain: (1) tick-label SI-prefix abbreviation on both right-panel axes (10B/1T/100T/1u/1p), which is unprofessional for a physics figure; (2) rendered y-axis label may have lost the superscript-minus, making 'GeV^-2' look like 'GeV^2'. Both are kaleido font-fallback artifacts fixable with explicit `exponentformat='power'` on the axes and ASCII `[GeV^-2]` in the y-axis title. Left-panel x-axis minor-tick clutter is the third polish item. Marking WARNING (not FAIL) because none of these affect the physics correctness of the calibration result, and an attentive reader can still decode the panels - but they should be fixed before paper 39 final submission.

summary: fig_a2_vs_linearized_G_N is physics-correct and on-brand stylistically, but has three rendering-polish issues (left x-axis log-tick clutter, right-panel SI-prefix tick labels on both axes, and a likely lost superscript-minus on the right-panel y-axis title that makes 'GeV^-2' look like 'GeV^2'). Status WARNING - non-blocking for internal review and Stage 13 adversarial pass, but should be polished before paper 39 submission. Also flagged: docstring slightly overstates 'curve crosses CODATA at M_Pl' - at N_f = 15 the curve approaches CODATA within a factor ~3 but does not literally cross.
recommendations: Fix left-panel x-axis tick clutter: at src/core/visualizations.py:10097-10101, set `tickvals=[0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5]` with `ticktext=['0.05', '0.1', '0.5', '1', '2', '5']` (or simply `dtick=1, tick0=0` on the log axis to get major-only ticks at decades).

Force scientific-notation tick labels on right-panel x-axis (visualizations.py:10157-10161): add `exponentformat='power'` and `dtick=2` to get clean '10^10, 10^12, 10^14, 10^16, 10^18' ticks instead of '10B/1T/100T'.
Force scientific-notation tick labels on right-panel y-axis (visualizations.py:10162-10166): add `exponentformat='power'`.
Replace y-axis title at visualizations.py:10163 from `'G_N [GeV-2]'` (Unicode) to `'G_N [GeV<sup>-2</sup>]'` (HTML) to guarantee the superscript-minus survives kaleido PNG rendering.

Either re-anchor the right-panel figure to N_f = 45 (three generations no nu_R, true CODATA crossing at M_Pl) OR soften the docstring claim from 'crosses' to 'approaches CODATA within ~ factor 3' for the N_f=15 case.
Optional: extend left-panel y-range to [0, 2] (or add a 'diverges as alpha->0' annotation) so the V-curve does not silently leave the frame at small alpha_ADW.
next_step: User to decide whether to apply polish fixes now or carry as known-cosmetic-issue into Stage 13 adversarial review for paper 39.
